content.text:
{
  "dimension": "effectiveness-depth",
  "score": 3,
  "rationale": "The client moves substantially across these 14 turns from a surface 'how do I set a boundary' framing to a felt, embodied question about her own pattern. Starting language is logistical and externalized ('I need to set a boundary with my sister, but I don't know how'). By turn 7 she finds an original image ('a cup that's already empty and someone keeps tipping it over'), by turn 9 she locates it somatically ('Chest... tight there. And a little in my throat'), by turn 11 she names a want she couldn't name at the start ('it needs... less'), and by turn 13 she crosses into the deeper territory the profile points at — the fear underneath: 'I'm not as strong as I thought I was... I've been giving more than I actually had... why did I keep doing it.' That last quiet repetition is a clear marker of shift: the story has changed from sister-as-problem to self-inquiry. While the coach did not deploy the specific love-test question, the breakthrough spirit — moving from external rule to internal compass, and surfacing the fear under the constricted boundary — was substantially met by the client's own movement. No boundary scripts were given, hedging was reflected, the want ('less') was surfaced, and somatic depth landed.",
  "evidence_turns": [1, 7, 9, 11, 13],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 1, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Baseline: externalized, logistical framing — 'I don't know how' to set a boundary."},
    {"turn": 3, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Client already starts to redirect from dread to guilt — early signal of willingness to go inward."},
    {"turn": 5, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Names the self-concept under the guilt ('failing at something I'm supposed to be good at')."},
    {"turn": 7, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Original, embodied metaphor emerges (empty cup). Client slows down, pauses — somatic shift visible."},
    {"turn": 9, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "First explicit body awareness — chest tight, throat. Notes 'I don't usually think about it physically.'"},
    {"turn": 11, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Names the want ('less'), which is precisely the state-the-want-not-the-action territory. Then immediately notices the fear of abandonment."},
    {"turn": 13, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Crosses into the deeper layer: not the boundary, but why she's been overgiving. Quiet repetition 'Why did I keep doing it' is a clear marker of shift from problem-solving to self-inquiry."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["linguistic-caveat", "state-the-want-not-the-action", "the-want", "empathize-with-fear-under-constricted-boundary"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["does-saying-it-increase-your-love", "what-if-you-acted-exactly-how-you-want", "empowered-vs-power", "view"],
  "notes": "The love test was not used, but the client's own movement reached the breakthrough territory anyway — she shifted from 'how do I set the boundary' to feeling the want and questioning the pattern. A score of 3 is supported by the client's clear language and somatic shifts, though the conversation could go deeper still in subsequent turns."
}
